overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a robust academic and research-focused presence across three social media platforms: LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon. With a combined follower count of 3,580 and 7 recent posts, the overall engagement rate stands at approximately 11.9%. The content primarily revolves around research publications, grants, academic opportunities (hiring, PhD), and discussions on disinformation and computational methods. While LinkedIn boasts the largest follower base and highest raw interaction volume, Bluesky demonstrates the strongest engagement relative to its follower count.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, research findings, tool releases (e.g., CooRnet v2.0), and general research updates.
Posts related to grant awards, hiring postdoctoral researchers, and opportunities for PhD students.
Discussions on methodological approaches, detecting coordinated link sharing, information operations, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and computational social science.
Announcements of upcoming presentations at conferences related to disinformation and computational methods.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high engagement rate by cross-posting key research announcements and fostering more interactive discussions there. Consider running Q&A sessions or polls related to recent publications.

Continue to utilize LinkedIn for formal announcements such as grant awards and hiring opportunities, as it consistently generates high raw interaction volume and reaches a broad professional audience. Explore LinkedIn Live for research presentations or Q&A.

Maintain consistent content across platforms, tailoring the tone and depth to each audience. For example, Mastodon could be used for more in-depth methodological discussions, while Bluesky and LinkedIn offer broader reach for major announcements.
Actively engage with comments and replies on posts, especially those announcing opportunities, to build community and encourage further interaction.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest engagement rate per follower (18.4%), indicating a highly active and responsive audience. It's effective for timely announcements of new papers and academic opportunities, suggesting it's a valuable platform for direct academic community interaction.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon serves as a platform for sharing more technical updates, like software releases (CooRnet v2.0) and methodological discussions. While its follower count is the lowest, it maintains a respectable engagement rate (9.1%), likely catering to a niche academic audience interested in specific research details.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower base (1,850) and generates the highest total interactions. It is exceptionally effective for formal, high-impact announcements such as grant awards and hiring calls, reinforcing its role as a professional networking and career platform. Its engagement rate (9.7%) is solid given the larger audience size.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
